title: bupleurum commutatum f. laxum
intro: Bupleurum commutatum f. laxum, also known as Chinese kochia or loose-branched bupleurum, is a variant of the Bupleurum commutatum plant, a type of flowering plant in the carrot family. Traditionally, it has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Bupleurum commutatum f. laxum include reducing fever, relieving liver and gallbladder disorders, and alleviating symptoms of depression and anxiety. In herbal preparations, Bupleurum commutatum f. laxum is often combined with other herbs and used in teas, capsules, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
